_comment: Surface (I) per-level curation (W2.E8.T2; the exit-demo level, outdoor snow). Token ids + routing choices only -- no ROM data, committable. Top-5 hue-ID heroes (RemasterFX=0 pin, coverage): tok1267 28.2% (tiled snow ground), tok1263 15.5% (bright snow bank), tok1200 5.2% / tok1201 4.2% / tok1199 2.0% (thin 64x17 dark tree/cliff bands on the hillsides -> ride the automatic whole_image AI-upscale tree; they are dark ~95 luma, NOT snow). The pink/orange sunset sky is a separate skyRender backdrop, not a hero token. tok2228 is pinned seam_safe to match dam's global curation (shared pack, global token ids). 2026-07-10: both snow heroes re-curated procedural->cc0_import (ambientCG Snow010A, CC0): A/B close-ups showed the photo material reads cleaner than the W2.E5.T1 snow synth (whose bank rendition mottles like dirt speckle), and cc0_import is Tier A1 (distributable) where tone=match synth was B. Built manually via tools/texpack/cc0_import.py (build_pack.py defers cc0_import); commands recorded per-token below. See docs/design/remaster-aaa/02-hd-asset-pipeline.md sec 4.2/4.7. 2026-07-10 M3 environment sweep: 34 more heroes re-curated ->cc0_import (Snow010A/Ice004/Concrete034/WoodSiding001/CorrugatedSteel005/Metal029/Metal032/MetalPlates006/Rust004/Planks003/Planks021/Wood026/Wood051), identified via multi-vantage dumps + hue-ID (pads 1/10/20/30/60/79); character textures now PINNED STOCK (see the per-token notes: per-frame re-upload perf tax + art integrity; jackets tok1785/1786 were never built) and fmt3/IA cutouts left on the AI tree; tok0022/tok0291 left alone (Dam-shared curation).

tok1267._note: Surface snow ground (hero #1, 64x32 fmt=4 I-format tileable=1 room, avgRGB bcbcbc). Build with cc0_import.py using size+args verbatim. mean 188 = the committed avgRGB (not ROM stats). highpass keeps the photo tile-uniform across the big tiled snowfield; i_alpha re-attaches the I-format intensity-as-alpha coupling (the loader's stbi path would otherwise force alpha=255). Tier derives from license (CC0 -> A1).

tok1263._note: Surface bright snow bank/slope (hero #2, 32x32 fmt=2 CI tileable=0 room, avgRGB dadae0 ~218,218,225 -- bright white snow, not the pink sky). Build with cc0_import.py using size+args verbatim. Non-tileable so no highpass needed; CI format keeps opaque alpha. Contrast 0.8 tames the photo's relief so the bank reads as clean bright snow. Tier derives from license (CC0 -> A1).

tok2228._note: Curation correction, harmonized with dam.json (global token, shared pack). A tileable=1 non-'room' token; the auto tree routes it whole_image, which fails validate_pack's seam self-check. Pin seam_safe so the HD tile still wraps and both levels' builds agree.

tok0033._note: Weathered exterior siding (I-format). Build with cc0_import.py using size+args verbatim. Tier derives from license (CC0 -> A1).

tok1265._note: Frozen creek running the length of the valley (13.6%% at pad 10). Cracked-ice CC0 sheet, desaturated 0.35 + contrast 0.65 so it reads icy gray-blue against the snow instead of loud teal. Build with cc0_import.py using size+args verbatim. Tier derives from license (CC0 -> A1).

tok0022._note: Dam ground/gravel hero surface. tok0022 is 64x32 tileable=0 room; the automatic tree would AI-upscale it, but the shipped Dam pack uses the FFT band-pass gravel synth (roadmap sec 3 'Dam learnings'). tone=match -> Tier B (tone-locked). [harmonized verbatim with the owning level override -- shared global token]

tok0291._note: Dam gray wall (hero #4, 64x64 tileable=1 room, avgRGB 545454). A flat tileable gray wall: a seamless tone-matched concrete synth reads cleaner than an ESRGAN upscale of a 64x64 tile (which mushes), and it exercises the W2.E5.T1 concrete preset. tone=match -> Tier B (tone-locked). [harmonized verbatim with the owning level override -- shared global token]

tok0265._note: Train dark floor/shadow band (hero #2, 64x32 fmt=3 IA tileable=1 room, avgRGB 101010). Tileable -> seam_safe AI upscale (auto choice, pinned; preserves the IA alpha and wraps). [harmonized verbatim with the owning level override -- shared global token]

tok1195._note: See-through riveted grate (alpha cutout, 32x32 tileable). Pinned whole_image so the dump alpha rides through; the auto small-tile rule would route it stock, and a CC0 plate import sealed the holes (validate_pack alpha-dropped catch, 2026-07-10).

tok2674._note: KF7 Soviet wooden stock/handguard (draw_class=weapon, 32x32). Rich walnut; the receiver plates tok0027/0028 stay on the AI tree because their painted bolt/pin detail must survive. Build with cc0_import.py using size+args verbatim. Tier derives from license (CC0 -> A1).

tok1198._note: Hillside treeline band (64x17 stock; ESRGAN melts it). First-party gen_treeline.py spruce art, V-flipped for the hillside geometry mapping, vertically wrap-faded because the faces tile the strip. alpha_cutout: the stock strip is a translucent XLU overlay with see-through gaps between trees (validate_pack alpha-dropped catch) -- alpha = tree coverage. Rebuild with gen_treeline.py using size+args verbatim. Tier A1 (first-party original).

tok1199._note: Hillside treeline band (64x17 stock; ESRGAN melts it). First-party gen_treeline.py spruce art, V-flipped for the hillside geometry mapping, vertically wrap-faded because the faces tile the strip. alpha_cutout: the stock strip is a translucent XLU overlay with see-through gaps between trees (validate_pack alpha-dropped catch) -- alpha = tree coverage. Rebuild with gen_treeline.py using size+args verbatim. Tier A1 (first-party original).

tok1200._note: Hillside treeline band (64x17 stock; ESRGAN melts it). First-party gen_treeline.py spruce art, V-flipped for the hillside geometry mapping, vertically wrap-faded because the faces tile the strip. Rebuild with gen_treeline.py using size+args verbatim. Tier A1 (first-party original).

tok1201._note: Hillside treeline band (64x17 stock; ESRGAN melts it). First-party gen_treeline.py spruce art, V-flipped for the hillside geometry mapping, vertically wrap-faded because the faces tile the strip. Left 42% reserved as bare snow slope matching the stock transition. Rebuild with gen_treeline.py using size+args verbatim. Tier A1 (first-party original).

tok0901._note: CHARACTER texture (guard skin/hands/ushanka fur/face; the manifest mislabels them draw_class=room). Pinned stock for BOTH art integrity and measured perf: animated characters re-upload their textures continuously, so an HD hit is a per-frame decode tax -- Dam pack-on went 120% of pack-off with these built and 85-103% without (median-of-5 interleaved perf_census, 2026-07-10).
